assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md, the summary table shows 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met'. The Correctness section details each criterion individually: Criterion 1 (single license filter) PASS, Criterion 2 (comma-separated filter) PASS, Criterion 3 (400 validation) PASS, Criterion 4 (pagination integration) PASS, Criterion 5 (unchanged response shape) PASS. Each criterion file (criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md) also ends with 'Verdict: PASS'.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md, the summary table shows 'Scope Containment | PASS | All 3 files match task specification exactly'. The Intent Alignment section contains a file-by-file table: 'modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs | Files to Modify | MATCH', 'modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs | Files to Modify | MATCH', 'tests/api/package.rs | Files to Create | MATCH'. It states: 'All files in the diff are accounted for in the task's "Files to Modify" and "Files to Create" lists. No extra files were changed, and no specified files are missing from the diff.'

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md, the summary table shows 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on PR'. The Root-Cause Investigation row shows 'N/A | No sub-tasks created'. The Style/Conventions section states 'Convention Upgrade: N/A -- no review comment suggestions to evaluate.' No evidence in any output file of sub-task creation.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report files: report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md. All files are analysis/verification documents. The report concludes with '*This comment was AI-generated by [sdlc-workflow/verify-pr]*' indicating it is a report. No code files appear in the outputs. The criterion files contain code snippets only as quoted evidence from the diff, not as modifications.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: The report.md presents findings in a structured table with 'Overall: PASS' verdict but does not contain any merge action, merge command, or auto-merge indication. It ends with '*This comment was AI-generated by [sdlc-workflow/verify-pr]*' consistent with a report for human review. No output file contains merge operations or gh pr merge commands.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md, the summary table shows 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, credentials, or sensitive data detected'. The Security section provides a detailed breakdown: 'Passwords / secrets | PASS -- no hardcoded passwords or secret values', 'API keys | PASS -- no API keys or tokens', 'Private keys | PASS -- no PEM/RSA/SSH private key material', 'Environment files (.env) | PASS -- no .env files added or modified', 'Cloud credentials (AWS/GCP/Azure) | PASS -- no cloud credential patterns', 'Database credentials | PASS -- no connection strings or DB passwords'.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md, the summary table shows 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | New test file `tests/api/package.rs` created with 4 tests; no existing tests removed or modified'. The Style/Conventions section further elaborates: 'ADDITIVE -- a new test file `tests/api/package.rs` is created with 4 integration tests. No existing test files were modified or removed.'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md, the summary table contains all specified rows: Scope Containment (PASS), Diff Size (PASS), Commit Traceability (PASS) under Intent Alignment section; Sensitive Patterns (PASS) under Security section; CI Status (PASS), Acceptance Criteria (PASS), Verification Commands (N/A) under Correctness section; Test Quality (PASS, combining 'Repetitive Test Detection: PASS', 'Test Documentation: PASS', 'Eval Quality: N/A') and Test Change Classification (ADDITIVE) under Style/Conventions section. All four domains are represented with their respective sub-verdicts.

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment: report.md contains a file-by-file table comparing each diff file to task specification (3 rows with MATCH status), plus diff size analysis per file (~20 lines in list.rs, ~10 lines in service/mod.rs, ~80 lines in tests/api/package.rs). Security: 6-category pattern scan table (Passwords, API keys, Private keys, .env files, Cloud credentials, Database credentials). Correctness: 5 detailed criterion analyses with code-level evidence including specific Rust code (e.g., 'Condition::any().add(package_license::Column::License.is_in(...))'), test assertions (e.g., 'body.items.iter().all(|p| p.license == "MIT")'), plus 5 separate criterion files with even more detail. Style/Conventions: 4-row test function table showing each test's distinct logic, doc comment listing for all 4 tests.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md, the Test Quality row states: 'Eval Quality: N/A -- no eval result reviews found'. The Style/Conventions section elaborates: 'N/A -- no eval result reviews found (no comments from `github-actions[bot]` with `## Eval Results` marker)'. The Test Quality combination verdict is PASS, derived from Repetitive Test Detection (PASS) and Test Documentation (PASS), with Eval Quality as N/A not affecting the result.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: Eval Quality is N/A as shown in report.md: 'Eval Quality: N/A -- no eval result reviews found'. No output file contains any reference to eval failure sub-task creation. The Root-Cause Investigation row in the summary table states 'N/A | No sub-tasks created', confirming no sub-tasks of any kind were created.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md, the summary table shows these as the first two rows, before any domain sections: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on PR' and 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created'. These appear at lines 5-6 of the report, before the Intent Alignment (line 22), Security (line 47), Correctness (line 63), and Style/Conventions (line 97) domain sections, indicating they are orchestrator-level verdicts determined independently from the four domain sub-agents.
